title: Arena Forge
blurb: A mobile-first auto-battler: draft magical constructs, forge duplicates into stronger versions, and outlast seven bots.
description: An original single-canvas auto-battler built as a pure-TypeScript Axiom app. Assemble a warband of magical constructs in a travelling forge arena, then alternate shop and combat rounds against seven deterministic bots — buying, positioning, and forging duplicate units into stronger versions — until one competitor is left standing. Units and abilities are data: thirty-six cards drive a small effect DSL the combat interpreter executes, so the roster is authored rather than coded. The simulation is DOM-free and renderer-free, which means the identical rules run in the browser, under the Node test runner, in the replay tool, or on a future authoritative server; matches are deterministic end to end and replayable from a seed. Designed for a phone in landscape first, and playable with a mouse on desktop.
